metadata.purpose: Catalog of all training session types for HYROX plan generation

running.name: Zone 2 Easy Run
running.purpose: Aerobic base, mitochondrial density, capillary development
running.progression: Increase duration by 5-10 min per week
running.source: Dan Plews, Matt Fitzgerald, Rich Ryan

running.name: Long Aerobic Run
running.purpose: Endurance capacity, fat oxidation, mental resilience
running.progression: Add 5-10 min per week up to 90 min

running.name: Threshold Intervals
running.progression: Add 1 rep per 2 weeks OR reduce recovery by 15s

running.name: Compromised Running
running.intensity: Moderate-hard pace immediately after station work
running.purpose: Running economy under fatigue, neuromuscular adaptation
running.progression: Increase run pace OR add station volume
running.source: Dan Plews, Hunter McIntyre, Fathom Nutrition

running.name: Recovery Run/Walk
running.intensity: Very easy, could hold conversation easily
running.purpose: Active recovery, blood flow, mental break

strength.name: General Strength
strength.purpose: Build general strength foundation, injury prevention
strength.progression: Add 2.5-5kg per week OR add 1-2 reps

strength.name: Strength Endurance
strength.intensity: Moderate loads, 12-20 reps, shorter rest (60-90s), RPE 7-8
strength.purpose: Muscular endurance, lactate tolerance, HYROX station preparation
strength.progression: Reduce rest intervals OR increase reps OR add weight

strength.name: Power/Explosive Strength
strength.intensity: Moderate-heavy loads, 3-6 reps, explosive tempo, full recovery
strength.purpose: Rate of force development for sled push/pull, efficient station completion
strength.source: WOD Science: 'HYROX requires maximizing power production at moderate weights'

hyrox_specific.name: Station Complex
hyrox_specific.intensity: Race pace or slightly below, RPE 7-8
format_options: SkiErg 1000m → Sled Push 50m → Run 1km → Sled Pull 50m → Run 1km
Row 1000m → Farmer Carry 200m → Run 1km → Wall Balls 50 → Run 1km
hyrox_specific.progression: Add stations, increase to race weight, reduce rest

hyrox_specific.name: Full Race Simulation
hyrox_specific.intensity: 90-95% race effort OR exact race pace practice
hyrox_specific.purpose: Test fitness, practice pacing, build confidence, identify weaknesses
hyrox_specific.format: Complete 8 runs + 8 stations at race weights
hyrox_specific.frequency_note: Every 3-4 weeks during build phase. NOT weekly.
hyrox_specific.progression: Improve pacing consistency, reduce total time
hyrox_specific.source: All sources agree: max every 3-4 weeks

hyrox_specific.name: Partial Simulation (Half HYROX)
hyrox_specific.purpose: Race-specific fitness without full recovery cost
hyrox_specific.progression: Increase to race pace, add more stations

conditioning.name: Cross-Training (Bike/Swim/Erg)
conditioning.purpose: Aerobic development without running impact stress
conditioning.source: Frontiers paper: 'replace running with other HYROX-specific endurance activities'

conditioning.name: High-Intensity Interval Training
format_options: Tabata-style station work (20s on/10s off x 8)
conditioning.source: Frontiers paper: 'HIIT effectively improves VO2max with less interference'

recovery.name: Mobility/Stretching
recovery.purpose: Maintain range of motion, injury prevention

recovery.name: Complete Rest Day
recovery.source: Rich Ryan: 'at least 1 full rest day per week'
